design_philosophy.core_loop: Master gravity-reversal mechanic to navigate hazards, solve spatial puzzles, and engage enemies through positioning rather than firepower
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Spatial awareness under pressure—player must instantly reorient understanding of up/down while dodging projectiles and environmental hazards that activate/deactivate based on gravity state
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Pilot an advanced combat mech (M-308 Gunner) with reality-bending gravity manipulation to infiltrate and destroy a rogue defense station
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Entirely built around a single core mechanic (gravity reversal) applied consistently across all level design, enemy patterns, and boss encounters. Refuses to dilute focus with traditional weapons; combat becomes pure movement and positioning. Irem arcade DNA shows in steep-but-fair difficulty and pattern-recognition emphasis. Intentionally scalable via Expert Mode, treating console and arcade difficulty philosophies as separate design expressions.

difficulty.lives_system: Standard NES lives system implied, resets on death
difficulty.continues_system: Unlimited continues in Expert Mode to allow practice; standard mode implies limited continues typical of NES era
difficulty.checkpointing: Stage-based checkpoints (6 main stages + 2 acts each, stage 7 is boss rush); death likely returns to stage start
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High—Expert Mode is entirely optional unlock after normal completion, fundamentally rebalances entire game (removes power-ups, reconfigures enemy/hazard placement, introduces new boss attack patterns). Player can choose learning vs. mastery path.

progression.upgrade_system: Minimalist power-up inventory: Power Beam, Shield, Gravity Fireball (temporary invincibility during flip). Player holds only one at a time, emphasizing scarcity and positioning over accumulation.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Completion of normal mode unlocks Expert Mode—a comprehensive reworking with removed power-ups and reconfigured challenge
replayability_hooks: Expert Mode transforms entire game into brutally unforgiving variant
Perfect level memorization required for Expert Mode completion
Boss patterns change across modes, incentivizing re-engagement
Screen-wrapping mechanic and gravity flip combinations reward mastery exploration

world_building_techniques: Sci-fi premise establishes setting (2501, Solar System colonized)
Plot framing: rogue laser cannon on Pluto threatening Earth
M-308 Gunner mech identity as protagonist
Victory condition: reach base and trigger self-destruct
Post-game epilogue: mech granted immortality and guardian role

boss_design.philosophy: Each boss encounter designed to test mastery of gravity-reversal mechanic; attack patterns and arena geometry change based on player's gravitational orientation. Stage 7 is dedicated boss rush, demanding sequence memorization.
notable_bosses: Stage bosses across acts 1-6 (specific names not documented)
Stage 7 boss rush finale with multiple sequential encounters
Expert Mode introduces new and more complex attack patterns for all bosses

faction_indicators: M-308 Gunner as humanity's last hope/ultimate protector
danger_signals: Existential threat to Earth requiring desperate countermeasure
environmental_storytelling: Pluto outpost setting implies militarized Space Age infrastructure
Laser cannon architecture visualizes the threat
Traversal through hostile base environment reinforces infiltration narrative
Parallax scrolling depth creates sense of scale and advanced technology
